reasoning: The probability of "Marty Supreme" winning Best Picture at the 2026 Academy Awards is extremely low. While it is officially nominated and has received 9 Oscar nominations (with Timothée Chalamet as a frontrunner for Best Actor), it is not a realistic contender for Best Picture. The Best Picture race is heavily dominated by Paul Thomas Anderson's "One Battle After Another" (which swept major precursor awards including the DGA, Golden Globes, Critics Choice, and most crucially, the PGA awards on March 1, 2026) and Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" (which holds a record 16 nominations). The PGA and DGA are historically the strongest predictors for the Best Picture Oscar, and with "One Battle After Another" claiming both, "Marty Supreme" has virtually no path to victory. Pre-PGA expert analysis noted this as "the most predictable Oscar race in history", projecting Marty Supreme as a 3rd to 5th place finisher at best. The Polymarket trading price of 3% on Feb 19 aligns with these fundamentals, and following the PGA win for "One Battle After Another", the true probability is safely at the technical floor of 2%.
